title: Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple
tagline: Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada, popularly known as Peruviruthy Malanada or Malanada is a Mala Appooppan shrine in South India.
overview.description: Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada, popularly known as Peruviruthy Malanada or Malanada is a Mala Appooppan shrine in South India. (It is believed that the 'Sankalpa Moorthy' at Malanada is 'Duryodhanan'). It is located at Edakkad Ward (Kara) of Poruvazhy village in Kunnathoor Taluk of Kollam district (Kerala State), India. This place is the northern border of Kollam district which Pathanamthitta and Alappuzha districts share the boundaries.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Malanada firework disaster
topPlaces.description: The Malanada firework disaster was a disaster in which 33 people lost their lives after a fire broke out in the Malanada temple at Poruvazhy in Kollam district, Kerala, India, on 23 March 1990. An electric spark in the place where the fireworks where stored caused the explosion.

topPlaces.name: Kunnathur, Kerala
topPlaces.description: Kunnathur (Kunnattūr,കുന്നത്തൂർ) is a taluk in Kollam district in the Indian state of Kerala. The capital of Kunnathur taluk is Sasthamkotta.

topPlaces.name: Sooranad
topPlaces.description: Sooranad North is a village in Kollam district in the south west Indian state of Kerala. It is believed that Sooranad was ruled by King SOORAN and hence the name.

topPlaces.name: Kadampanad Bhagavathy Temple
topPlaces.description: Kadampanad Bhagavathy Temple is a Hindu temple situated at Kadampanad in Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India. It is 12 km from Adoor, 6.6 km from Mannady and 10 km from Sasthamkotta.

topPlaces.name: Kadampanad
topPlaces.description: Kadampanad grama panchayath is the southernmost part of Pathanamthitta district. It comes under Parakkode block of Adoor taluk.

topPlaces.name: St. Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Kadampanad
topPlaces.description: St. Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Kadampanad, popularly known as Kadampanad Church ( കടമ്പനാട് പള്ളി ) is an ancient church of Saint Thomas Christians situated in the Central Travancore village of Kadampanad, in Pathanamthitta District, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Vallikavu
topPlaces.description: Vallikavu is a small village in Kollam District of Kerala. Vallikavu is chiefly inhabited by farmers and fishermen.

topPlaces.name: Poruvazhy
topPlaces.description: Poruvazhy is a village in Kollam district in the state of Kerala, India. The Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple is in the village.

items.note: The Malanada firework disaster was a disaster in which 33 people lost their lives after a fire broke out in the Malanada temple at Poruvazhy in Kollam district, Kerala, India, on 23 March 1990. An electric spark in the place where the fireworks where stored caused the explosion.
Kunnathur (Kunnattūr,കുന്നത്തൂർ) is a taluk in Kollam district in the Indian state of Kerala. The capital of Kunnathur taluk is Sasthamkotta.
Sooranad North is a village in Kollam district in the south west Indian state of Kerala. It is believed that Sooranad was ruled by King SOORAN and hence the name.
Kadampanad Bhagavathy Temple is a Hindu temple situated at Kadampanad in Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India. It is 12 km from Adoor, 6.6 km from Mannady and 10 km from Sasthamkotta.
Kadampanad grama panchayath is the southernmost part of Pathanamthitta district. It comes under Parakkode block of Adoor taluk.

St. Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Kadampanad, popularly known as Kadampanad Church ( കടമ്പനാട് പള്ളി ) is an ancient church of Saint Thomas Christians situated in the Central Travancore village of Kadampanad, in Pathanamthitta District, Kerala, India.
Vallikavu is a small village in Kollam District of Kerala. Vallikavu is chiefly inhabited by farmers and fishermen.
Poruvazhy is a village in Kollam district in the state of Kerala, India. The Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple is in the village.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (75 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (76 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Malanada firework disaster, Kunnathur, Kerala, Sooranad, Kadampanad Bhagavathy Temple, Kadampanad.
